# Source Maps and Debug Symbols Competitor Review - 2026-06-13

This note records why LogBrew is currently behind Sentry and Datadog for post-deploy stack trace quality, and what we should build without copying their heavy default behavior.

## Sources Checked

- Sentry JavaScript source maps docs: https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/sourcemaps/
- Sentry CLI source maps upload docs: https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/sourcemaps/uploading/cli/
- Sentry JavaScript public repo at `getsentry/sentry-javascript@4e12c7a9013daa6b14e6b7e6106304e3eba42724`
- Sentry source paths read: `dev-packages/test-utils/src/sourcemap-upload-utils.ts`, `packages/sveltekit/src/vite/sourceMaps.ts`
- Datadog JavaScript source maps docs: https://docs.datadoghq.com/real_user_monitoring/guide/upload-javascript-source-maps/
- Datadog Source Maps build plugin docs: https://docs.datadoghq.com/real_user_monitoring/application_monitoring/browser/build_plugins/source_maps/
- Datadog CI public repo at `DataDog/datadog-ci@74ed439f292a09a44d38de1f4f5ac092e9528b75`
- Datadog source paths read: `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/README.md`, `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/upload.ts`, `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/interfaces.ts`, `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/validation.ts`, `packages/base/src/helpers/git/format-git-sourcemaps-data.ts`, `packages/base/src/commands/react-native/injectDebugId.ts`

2026-06-15 drift check: Sentry's current docs still recommend Debug IDs for matching event stack frames to minified source and source maps, and Datadog now documents a browser build plugin that discovers `.js`/`.map` pairs and uploads them with Git metadata during builds. LogBrew should still keep runtime SDKs dependency-light, but the future public tooling path should include an optional build-plugin layer after backend upload/lookup exists.

2026-06-17 React Native bundle follow-up: re-read Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@74ed439f292a09a44d38de1f4f5ac092e9528b75` paths `packages/base/src/commands/react-native/injectDebugId.ts`, `upload.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, `utils.ts`, `validation.ts`, and React Native fixtures such as `basic-android/index.android.bundle(.map)` and `basic-ios/main.jsbundle(.map)`. Also re-read Sentry React Native `getsentry/sentry-react-native@580fb5c7bf39cc1a8caf7a30af9078c887eb40b9` paths `packages/core/sentry.gradle.kts`, `packages/core/scripts/sentry-xcode.sh`, `packages/core/scripts/copy-debugid.js`, `packages/core/scripts/has-sourcemap-debugid.js`, and `packages/expo-upload-sourcemaps/cli.js`. Pattern: competitors do not assume React Native bundles end in `.js`; Android commonly uses `index.android.bundle`, iOS uses `main.jsbundle`, and the paired source map is often the bundle path plus `.map`. LogBrew's dry-run scripts now scan `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bundle`, and `.jsbundle`, use sibling `.map` fallback for bundle outputs, and still avoid upload or runtime symbolication claims until backend support exists.

2026-06-17 native/mobile artifact follow-up: read Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/commands/proguard/upload.rs`, `src/utils/proguard/upload.rs`, `src/utils/proguard/mapping.rs`, `src/commands/debug_files/upload.rs`, and `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`; Sentry Android Gradle Plugin `getsentry/sentry-android-gradle-plugin@a5a55f8448cddd9e0282a89c436b339704b30ab3` paths `plugin-build/src/main/kotlin/io/sentry/android/gradle/tasks/SentryGenerateProguardUuidTask.kt`, `SentryUploadProguardMappingsTask.kt`, `SentryUploadNativeSymbolsTask.kt`, `SentryTasksProvider.kt`, and `extensions/SentryPluginExtension.kt`; Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@74ed439f292a09a44d38de1f4f5ac092e9528b75` paths `packages/base/src/commands/dsyms/upload.ts`, `dsyms/utils.ts`, `dsyms/interfaces.ts`, `elf-symbols/upload.ts`, `unity-symbols/upload.ts`, and `unity-symbols/interfaces.ts`. Pattern: mature competitors keep native symbols in build/deploy tools, expose dry-run/no-upload modes, compute stable IDs or UUID/build IDs, default native source inclusion off, and avoid runtime SDK ownership. LogBrew now adds `scripts/create_native_release_artifact_manifest.py` plus `scripts/real_user_native_release_artifact_smoke.sh` for conservative iOS dSYM, Android ProGuard/R8, and Android native `.so` dry-run manifests with relative paths, stable content-derived artifact IDs, and no artifact upload/symbolication claim.

2026-06-17 Android native symbol follow-up: re-read Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@74ed439f292a09a44d38de1f4f5ac092e9528b75` paths `packages/base/src/commands/elf-symbols/elf.ts`, `upload.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, and `elf-constants.ts`, plus Sentry Android native-symbol upload task and Sentry CLI debug-file upload command listed above. Datadog's useful pattern is to reject non-ELF files, unsupported executable/shared-library types, unsupported architectures, files with no GNU/Go build ID or code hash, and files with no debug info or symbol tables; it records symbol source as `debug_info`, `symbol_table`, or `dynamic_symbol_table`. LogBrew now implements a dependency-free subset for Android `.so` dry-run validation: ELF64/ELF32 header parsing, section-name lookup, GNU/Go build ID note extraction, Android ABI architecture mapping, code-hash fallback only when `.text` exists, and symbol-source reporting without serializing symbol bytes.

2026-06-17 dSYM UUID follow-up: re-read Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@74ed439f292a09a44d38de1f4f5ac092e9528b75` paths `packages/base/src/commands/dsyms/upload.ts`, `dsyms/utils.ts`, and `dsyms/interfaces.ts`, plus Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`. Datadog shells out to `dwarfdump --uuid`, parses `UUID: ... (arch) ...` rows, thins multi-arch dSYMs with `lipo`, names compressed bundles by UUID, and uploads UUID metadata. Sentry parses debug files through `symbolic`, records `Object::debug_id()`, and treats stable debug identifiers as the symbolication join key. LogBrew now implements a dependency-free dry-run subset for dSYM DWARF objects: Mach-O and fat Mach-O magic detection, `LC_UUID` extraction, architecture mapping, per-file UUID metadata, and warnings for missing UUIDs without serializing symbol bytes or invoking Apple tooling.

2026-06-19 dSYM archive follow-up: refreshed current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/dsyms/upload.ts`, `dsyms/utils.ts`, and `dsyms/interfaces.ts`, plus Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/commands/debug_files/upload.rs`, `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`, and `src/utils/dif.rs`. Functions/classes read: Datadog `DsymsUploadCommand.prepareSearchDirectory`, `findDsyms`, `parseDwarfdumpOutput`, `thinDsym`, `compressDsyms`, `zipDirectoryToArchive`, `unzipArchiveToDirectory`, and `CompressedDsym.asMultipartPayload`; Sentry `allow_zips`, `search_difs`, and `walk_difs_directory`. Pattern: mature tools accept zipped dSYM input, expand archives to find `.dSYM` bundles, key them by UUID/debug ID, and upload compressed symbol archives from build tooling. LogBrew now mirrors the safe local subset: `ios_dsym=<archive.zip>` validates safe archive entries, discovers nested `.dSYM/Contents/Resources/DWARF/*` files, extracts only temporary DWARF bytes for `LC_UUID` metadata, records `archive.zip!entry` paths, keeps the ZIP as one upload part, and blocks unsafe archive paths without echoing them. This remains dry-run/loopback proof, not backend upload or symbolication.

2026-06-17 Breakpad symbol follow-up: re-read current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/pe-symbols/breakpad.ts`, `upload.ts`, and `__tests__/pe.test.ts`, plus Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` `src/utils/dif.rs`. Datadog parses ASCII Breakpad `MODULE` headers, normalizes GUID/age identifiers, maps CPU to architecture, infers `debug_info` when `FILE` records appear before `FUNC`/`PUBLIC`, and uploads the `.sym` as a PE-symbol source. Sentry treats Breakpad as a debug-info format through `symbolic` and records debug IDs/features. LogBrew now implements the dry-run subset: `.sym` discovery, MODULE validation, GUID/age/source metadata, basename-only module names, unknown-CPU warnings, relative paths, and no FILE/FUNC/PUBLIC/source-path serialization.

2026-06-17 PDB/PE symbol follow-up: re-read current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/pe-symbols/pe.ts`, `pe-constants.ts`, `upload.ts`, and `__tests__/pe.test.ts`, plus Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/utils/dif.rs` and `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`. Datadog parses PE `MZ`/`PE` headers, maps machine architecture, reads CodeView `RSDS` GUID/age/PDB filename metadata, and uploads the associated sibling `.pdb`; Sentry treats PDB and Portable PDB as debug-info formats and fixes PDB age from PE metadata when needed. LogBrew now implements the dry-run subset as `dotnet_pdb`: PE `.dll`/`.exe` discovery, CodeView GUID/age extraction, sibling `.pdb` presence checks, basename-only PDB names, relative paths, and no PDB payload or embedded local PDB path serialization.

2026-06-17 native artifact limit follow-up: refreshed Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/elf-symbols/upload.ts` and `packages/base/src/commands/pe-symbols/upload.ts`, Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`, `src/utils/dif_upload/error.rs`, and `src/constants.rs`, and Sentry Android Gradle Plugin `getsentry/sentry-android-gradle-plugin@f62e071eea2e6db2b0e646fcbd4f0dddd17fffb5` paths `SentryUploadNativeSymbolsTask.kt` and `SentryPluginExtension.kt`. Functions/classes read: Datadog `ElfSymbolsUploadCommand.getElfSymbolFiles`, `removeBuildIdDuplicates`, `performElfSymbolsUpload`, `PESymbolsUploadCommand.getPESymbolFiles`, `removeBuildIdDuplicates`, and `performPESymbolsUpload`; Sentry `DifUpload::new`, `valid_size`, `validate_dif`, `filter_bad_sources`, and `ValidationError::TooLarge`; Sentry Android `SentryUploadNativeSymbolsTask.getArguments` and `SentryPluginExtension` native-symbol options. Pattern: Sentry hard-fails oversized debug files with a default 2GB DIF cap and keeps source inclusion opt-in; Datadog keeps dry-run/no-upload and bounded concurrency, then deduplicates repeated ELF/PE build IDs by preferring richer symbol sources. LogBrew now mirrors the safe dry-run subset without upload: native manifests emit explicit `limits`, default to 500 candidate symbol files and 2GB per symbol file/artifact with CLI overrides, block oversized or over-count artifacts, and dedupe Android native `.so` identities by keeping the richer symbol source. ELF parsing moved into `scripts/native_release_artifact_elf.py` so the main manifest script shrank instead of growing.

2026-06-17 Unity symbol follow-up: refreshed Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/unity-symbols/upload.ts`, `helpers.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, `README.md`, and `__tests__/upload.test.ts`; Sentry Unity `getsentry/sentry-unity@4cda4392328f489a0b22490f0f0a2cc401cfc622` paths `src/Sentry.Unity.Editor/Android/DebugSymbolUpload.cs` and `test/Sentry.Unity.Editor.Tests/Android/DebugSymbolUploadTests.cs`; and Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs` and `src/constants.rs`. Functions/classes read: Datadog `UnitySymbolsUploadCommand.verifyParameters`, `getBuildId`, `performSoUpload`, `performIl2CppMappingUpload`, and `getMappingMetadata`; Sentry Unity `DebugSymbolUpload.AppendUploadToGradleFile`, `TryCopySymbolsToGradleProject`, `GetSymbolUploadPaths`, and `GetMappingFilePath`; Sentry CLI `walk_difs_zip`, `walk_difs_directory`, `create_il2cpp_mappings`, `valid_size`, and `validate_dif`. Pattern: competitors keep Unity symbols in build tooling, require release/build identity, upload Android `.so` files and IL2CPP `LineNumberMappings.json` separately, support dry-run/no-upload checks, and treat source inclusion/upload as explicit build-step behavior. LogBrew now adds a dependency-free `unity_symbols` dry-run manifest: it requires a safe `build_id`, validates Unity `.so` files through the shared ELF rules, records optional `LineNumberMappings.json` as metadata-only, warns when managed mapping data is missing, and keeps source paths, method names, symbol bytes, query strings, and upload-only auth material out of manifests. This still is not upload, storage, lookup, or symbolication support.

2026-06-18 Unity archive follow-up: re-read current Sentry Unity `getsentry/sentry-unity@a9bebf56a8808b866ff11330bf42030685701cf9` paths `src/Sentry.Unity.Editor/Android/DebugSymbolUpload.cs` and `src/Sentry.Unity.Editor/Native/BuildPostProcess.cs`, plus Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/unity-symbols/upload.ts` and `interfaces.ts`. Functions/classes read: Sentry `DebugSymbolUpload.AppendUploadToGradleFile`, `TryCopySymbolsToGradleProject`, `GetSymbolUploadPaths`, `GetMappingFilePath`, and native post-process `debug-files upload --il2cpp-mapping` command construction; Datadog `UnitySymbolsUploadCommand.getBuildId`, `performSoUpload`, `performIl2CppMappingUpload`, and mapping metadata types. Pattern: Sentry and Datadog accept Unity symbol material as build artifacts, often from copied/exported directories or tool-owned ZIP/debug-file walking, then upload native `.so` files and IL2CPP mappings from build tooling. LogBrew now mirrors only the safe local subset for ZIP archives: `unity_symbols=<symbols.zip>` validates safe archive entry names, reads exactly one `build_id`, validates extracted `.so` entries through existing ELF rules, records optional `LineNumberMappings.json` as metadata-only, reports archive-entry paths as `archive.zip!entry`, blocks unsafe archive paths without echoing them, and keeps source paths, method names, symbol bytes, and backend upload claims out of manifests.

2026-06-18 PE/Breakpad duplicate follow-up: re-read Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/pe-symbols/upload.ts`, `breakpad.ts`, and `pe.ts`, plus Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` paths `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs` and `src/utils/dif.rs`. Functions/classes read: Datadog `PESymbolsUploadCommand.getPESymbolFiles`, `removeBuildIdDuplicates`, `getSymbolSourcePriority`, `performPESymbolsUpload`, `getBreakpadSymMetadata`, `getPEFileMetadata`, and `getBuildId`; Sentry `DifMatch.debug_id`, `fix_pdb_ages`, `extract_embedded_ppdb`, `validate_dif`, and `DifFile::try_open`. Pattern: Datadog deduplicates PE and Breakpad symbol sources by PDB GUID plus age and keeps the richer symbol source, while Sentry treats PE/PDB/PortablePDB/Breakpad as debug-file formats keyed by debug IDs. LogBrew now mirrors the safe dry-run subset for `breakpad_symbols` and `dotnet_pdb`: duplicate GUID/age identities collapse deterministically, `debug_info` beats `symbol_table`, same-quality duplicates keep the first relative path, and warnings describe the decision without serializing source paths, function names, PDB payloads, or symbol bytes. This still is not upload, storage, lookup, or symbolication support.

2026-06-18 Portable PDB payload follow-up: re-read current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/pe-symbols/pe.ts`, `upload.ts`, and `__tests__/pe.test.ts`; Sentry CLI `getsentry/sentry-cli@d9766db0b3c61cc6c0e445d8840c0765c42d6eea` `src/utils/dif_upload/mod.rs`; and Sentry symbolic `getsentry/symbolic@3581ad0d67a074e83ba10e612bce34a83e5916a8` paths `symbolic-debuginfo/src/pe.rs`, `symbolic-debuginfo/src/ppdb.rs`, and `symbolic-ppdb/src/format/{mod.rs,streams.rs,raw.rs}`. Functions/classes read: Datadog `getPEFileMetadata`, `getBuildId`, `getPESymbolFiles`, `getMappingMetadata`, and `removeBuildIdDuplicates`; Sentry/symbolic `PeObject::debug_id`, `PeObject::embedded_ppdb`, `PortablePdbObject::parse`, `PortablePdbObject::debug_id`, `PortablePdb::pdb_id`, `PdbStream::parse`, `PdbStream::id`, `collect_object_dif`, and `extract_embedded_ppdb`. Pattern: PE CodeView identity is not enough for mature tooling; Portable PDB payloads carry a `#Pdb` stream identity and embedded Portable PDBs can be extracted from PE files, while upload remains build-tool/backend-owned. LogBrew now mirrors the safe dry-run subset by parsing external Portable PDB `BSJB` metadata roots, reading only the `#Pdb` stream ID, cross-checking it against PE CodeView GUID/age, blocking malformed or mismatched Portable PDB payloads, warning when PDB payloads are unrecognized, and never serializing PDB payload bytes, embedded source paths, symbols, or source-link data.

2026-06-18 embedded Portable PDB follow-up: refreshed Sentry symbolic at `getsentry/symbolic@15e05ed4827c11ed410763de879757ef0246e4b8` paths `symbolic-debuginfo/src/pe.rs` and `symbolic-ppdb/src/format/{mod.rs,streams.rs,raw.rs}` after confirming current public heads for Sentry CLI and Datadog CI still match the previously recorded commits. Functions/classes read: `PeObject::embedded_ppdb`, `PeEmbeddedPortablePDB::get_size`, `PeEmbeddedPortablePDB::decompress_to`, `PortablePdb::parse`, `PdbStream::parse`, and `PdbStreamHeader`. Pattern: embedded Portable PDBs are PE debug-directory type 17 records with an `MPDB` header, declared uncompressed size, raw deflate payload, and Portable PDB `#Pdb` identity after decompression. LogBrew now mirrors only that validation subset: `dotnet_pdb` dry runs can accept a PE with a valid embedded Portable PDB when no sibling `.pdb` exists, cap embedded decompression, parse only the `#Pdb` identity, block malformed or mismatched embedded payloads, and keep compressed bytes, source-link data, embedded source paths, symbols, and payload contents out of manifests. This remains local dry-run validation, not upload, storage, lookup, or symbolication support.

2026-06-18 classic Windows PDB follow-up: refreshed Sentry symbolic at `getsentry/symbolic@15e05ed4827c11ed410763de879757ef0246e4b8` path `symbolic-debuginfo/src/pdb/mod.rs` and upstream Rust `pdb@0.8.0` paths `src/msf/mod.rs`, `src/pdb.rs`, `src/pdbi.rs`, and `src/dbi.rs`. Functions/classes read: Sentry `PdbObject::test`, `PdbObject::parse`, and `PdbObject::debug_id`; upstream `BigMSF::new`, `BigMSF::look_up_stream`, `PDB::pdb_information`, `PDB::debug_information`, `PDBInformation::parse`, and `DebugInformation::age`. Pattern: mature tooling recognizes MSF 7.00 classic PDBs, reads stream 1 for GUID/PDB age, prefers stream 3 DBI age when available because it better matches PE CodeView, and treats the resulting GUID/age as the debug identity. LogBrew now mirrors only that safe identity subset: `dotnet_pdb` dry runs parse bounded MSF stream directories, read only PDB information and DBI age bytes, cross-check classic Windows PDB debug IDs against PE CodeView, block malformed or mismatched payloads, and keep symbols, source streams, module records, file paths, and PDB bytes out of manifests. This remains local dry-run validation, not upload, storage, lookup, or symbolication support.

2026-06-18 Vite build-output follow-up: re-read current Sentry JavaScript `getsentry/sentry-javascript@ccf5571c78c1116ba15fa7186234c86dcf57db5a` paths `packages/sveltekit/src/vite/sourceMaps.ts` and `dev-packages/test-utils/src/sourcemap-upload-utils.ts`, plus current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/upload.ts`, `validation.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, and `helpers/git/format-git-sourcemaps-data.ts`. Functions/classes read: Sentry `makeCustomSentryVitePlugins`, `_getUpdatedSourceMapSettings`, custom `closeBundle`, `getFiles`, `detectSentryRelease`, and test helpers `getArtifactBundles`, `getDebugIdPairs`, `getSourcemaps`; Datadog `SourcemapsUploadCommand.execute`, `getMatchingSourcemapFiles`, `getLegacyMatchingSourcemapFiles`, `getMinifiedURLAndRelativePath`, `getPayloadsToUpload`, `upload`, `Sourcemap.asMultipartPayload`, `getMetadataPayload`, and `validatePayload`. Pattern: Sentry enables/keeps Vite source maps, defers upload until build output is complete, and can delete maps after upload; Datadog requires release/service/minified-path-prefix, discovers JS/map pairs, validates files, attaches Git metadata when opted in, supports dry-run, and retries uploads. LogBrew now improves the safe local half without backend claims: Debug ID prep has an explicit `--strip-sources-content` dry-run/write option, and `scripts/real_user_vite_release_artifact_smoke.sh` builds a real `vite@8.0.16` app with isolated npm cache, injects matching Debug IDs, strips Vite `sourcesContent`, proves the manifest is ready without `--allow-sources-content`, strips query/hash from minified URLs, and verifies the generated source map resolves a minified marker back to `src/main.js` without serializing raw source content. Vite 8.0.16 currently requires Node `^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0`, and its esbuild minifier path now requires explicit `esbuild@0.28.1`.

2026-06-18 Next.js build-output follow-up: re-read current Sentry JavaScript `getsentry/sentry-javascript@839391fecb10be6765f55c418197cad541a1784b` paths `packages/nextjs/src/config/getBuildPluginOptions.ts`, `packages/nextjs/src/config/handleRunAfterProductionCompile.ts`, `packages/nextjs/src/client/clientNormalizationIntegration.ts`, `packages/nextjs/src/config/webpack.ts`, and `dev-packages/test-utils/src/sourcemap-upload-utils.ts`; re-read current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/sourcemaps/upload.ts`, `validation.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, and `helpers/git/format-git-sourcemaps-data.ts`. Functions/classes read: Sentry `createSourcemapUploadAssetPatterns`, `createSourcemapUploadIgnorePattern`, `createFilesToDeleteAfterUploadPattern`, `shouldSkipSourcemapUpload`, `getBuildPluginOptions`, `handleRunAfterProductionCompile`, `stripSourceMappingURLComments`, `nextjsClientStackFrameNormalizationIntegration`, and test helpers `getDebugIdPairs`/`getSourcemaps`; Datadog `SourcemapsUploadCommand.execute`, `addRepositoryDataToPayloads`, `validatePayload`, `Sourcemap.asMultipartPayload`, `getMetadataPayload`, and `normalizeSources`. Pattern: Sentry treats Next source maps as framework build artifacts, scopes upload paths to `.next` server/static outputs, ignores known internal manifests, injects Debug IDs after production compile when needed, uploads after the framework output exists, optionally deletes maps, and normalizes client frame URLs to `app:///_next/...`; Datadog keeps the generic CLI model of explicit release/service/minified-path-prefix, validation, optional Git metadata, dry-run, and upload retries. LogBrew now proves the safe local subset for real `next@16.2.9` output without backend claims: `scripts/real_user_next_release_artifact_smoke.sh` builds a minimal App Router app with `productionBrowserSourceMaps`, validates `.next/static/chunks` artifacts, strips all Next `sourcesContent`, injects matching Debug IDs into minified chunks and maps, creates a ready manifest with `app:///_next/static/chunks` URLs stripped of query/hash text, and verifies a generated position maps back to `components/CheckoutProbe.jsx` without serializing raw source content. Next 16.2.9 currently requires Node `>=20.9.0`.

2026-06-18 React Native build-output follow-up: re-read current Sentry React Native `getsentry/sentry-react-native@78878471dc46a38eb72268f452a8abac8c1813a2` paths `packages/core/sentry.gradle.kts`, `packages/core/scripts/sentry-xcode.sh`, `packages/core/scripts/copy-debugid.js`, `packages/core/scripts/has-sourcemap-debugid.js`, and `packages/expo-upload-sourcemaps/cli.js`; re-read current Datadog CI `DataDog/datadog-ci@625da2306abd9ac25ffbc1b4a810beed8cee5ef9` paths `packages/base/src/commands/react-native/injectDebugId.ts`, `upload.ts`, `interfaces.ts`, `utils.ts`, and `validation.ts`. Functions/classes read: Sentry `extractBundleTaskArguments`, `extractBundleTaskArgumentsLegacy`, `forceSourceMapOutputFromBundleTask`, Gradle upload task setup, `copy-debugid.js`, `has-sourcemap-debugid.js`, `sentry-xcode.sh`, and Expo asset grouping/upload logic; Datadog `ReactNativeInjectDebugIdCommand.execute`, `injectDebugIds`, `injectDebugIdIntoFiles`, `injectDebugIdIntoBundle`, `injectDebugIdIntoSourceMap`, `createDebugIdSnippet`, `generateDebugId`, `ReactNativeUploadCommand.execute`, `getPayloadsToUpload`, `upload`, `RNSourcemap.asMultipartPayload`, `removeSourcesContentFromSourceMap`, `getBundleName`, and `validatePayload`. Pattern: Sentry forces or discovers Metro source-map output from Gradle/Xcode, copies Debug IDs from packager maps to Hermes maps, and delegates upload to Sentry CLI; Datadog explicitly targets `.bundle`/`.jsbundle` plus sibling `.map`, requires release/service/platform/build metadata, supports dry-run/debug-ID injection, and can remove `sourcesContent` before upload. LogBrew now proves the safe local subset for a real `react-native@0.86.0` Android Metro bundle using explicit `@react-native-community/cli@20.1.3`, `@react-native/metro-config@0.86.0`, `react@19.2.7`, and Metro 0.84.4: `scripts/real_user_react_native_release_artifact_smoke.sh` builds `index.android.bundle` plus `.map`, injects matching Debug IDs, strips `sourcesContent`, rewrites absolute Metro source paths under the app root to relative `sources`, creates a ready `app:///react-native/...` manifest without query/hash text, and verifies a generated position maps back to `index.js` without serializing raw source or local temp paths. Current React Native no longer exposes the bundle CLI from `react-native` alone, so the verifier records explicit CLI and Metro config dependencies.

This remains dry-run validation only, not backend upload, storage, lookup, or native symbolication.

## What Competitors Do Better Today

Sentry and Datadog both treat source maps and native symbols as release artifacts, not as normal runtime telemetry. The user uploads build output and metadata before or during deploy, then runtime errors carry enough release and artifact identity for the backend to unminify or symbolicate stack frames later.

Sentry's JavaScript source shows two useful patterns:

- Artifact bundles connect minified source and source maps with `debug-id` metadata, so lookup does not depend only on file URL strings.
- Framework helpers can alter build settings, upload after framework output is complete, warn when source maps are disabled, and optionally delete local source maps after upload.

Datadog's CLI source shows a complementary set of patterns:

- Upload requires service, release version, and minified path prefix, which makes runtime matching explicit and avoids relying on raw browser URLs alone.
- Payloads include minified file, source map, and metadata as separate multipart parts.
- Git metadata can be attached for source links, but users can disable git collection or provide repository URL and commit SHA explicitly.
- Validation checks for missing and empty source maps or minified files before upload.
- React Native gets an explicit debug-ID injection command for bundle/source-map matching.

## Where LogBrew Is Worse

LogBrew SDKs can capture errors and releases, and stack text is intentionally opt-in for privacy. That is safer than many defaults, but it is not enough to compete on production JavaScript or native crash triage:

- There is no public artifact upload API for source maps, dSYMs, ProGuard mappings, PDBs, Breakpad symbols, ELF debug files, or Unity symbols.
- There is no artifact identity field such as `debugId` or `artifactId` in public runtime guidance.
- There is no public build-time uploader or public backend upload CI example. Repo-local JavaScript, Vite, Next.js, React Native, and native/mobile dry-run tools now exist for manifest validation, Debug ID preparation, source-content stripping, source-prefix stripping, real build-output proof, local minified-frame source-map proof, loopback fake-intake JavaScript/Vite/Next/native upload proof, an installed `@logbrew/sdk` release-artifact CLI with local frame resolution, installed dependency-free Vite/Next/React Native local helpers, but they do not upload to a public backend or prove backend symbolication.
- There is no backend symbolication contract tying runtime issue frames to uploaded release artifacts.
- There is no real backend proof that a minified stack trace can be uploaded, looked up, and symbolicated without exposing source maps publicly.

## Product Direction

LogBrew should not copy heavyweight auto-instrumentation or upload raw source by default. The better LogBrew version should be explicit, auditable, and privacy-first:

- Make artifact upload separate from runtime SDK installation.
- Require explicit release, environment, service, and artifact identity.
- Default to dry-run and manifest previews in CLI/build tooling.
- Strip query strings and fragments from minified paths before matching.
- Treat `sourcesContent` as a sensitive opt-in, with a documented default to exclude or scrub source content where possible.
- Preserve Git metadata only when app-owned CI opts in, with `--disable-git` and explicit `repositoryUrl`/`commitSha` alternatives.
- Keep runtime SDK imports dependency-light; local source-map preparation may live behind an explicit installed CLI, while public upload belongs in a separate tool/package or framework-owned build plugin after backend support exists.

## Concrete LogBrew Work

- Define the backend contract before adding SDK claims: upload, list, delete, validation error, and symbolication lookup shapes.
- Keep expanding the JS dry-run path before real backend upload: validate minified files, React Native `.bundle`/`.jsbundle` outputs, source maps, matching Debug IDs, sensitive `sourcesContent`, source path prefixes, Git metadata, normalized minified URLs, minified-frame source-map resolution, installed package entrypoints, build-plugin ergonomics, and upload transport behavior. Local real-user smokes now prove synthetic web `.js`, synthetic React Native-style bundle output, real Vite 8 output through installed `@logbrew/sdk/vite-release-artifacts`, an actual thrown Vite minified frame resolved through installed `symbolicate-js`, real Next 16 App Router build output through installed `@logbrew/next/release-artifacts`, real Next 16 loopback upload retry through installed `upload-js`, real React Native 0.86 Metro bundle output through installed `@logbrew/react-native/release-artifacts`, local minified-frame resolution from both repo verifier and installed `@logbrew/sdk` CLI, installed release-artifact CLI use, and loopback fake-intake upload success/auth-failure/validation-failure/retry/tamper behavior, but public backend upload and symbolication still depend on the backend contract.
- Keep expanding native/mobile manifests and loopback upload proof before real upload: external Portable PDB, embedded Portable PDB, classic Windows PDB identity validation, Unity ZIP archive validation, and Unity ZIP loopback transport now exist, while richer PE/PDB and Breakpad edge-case validation, backend upload/storage/lookup, and platform-specific symbol metadata remain before public support claims.
- Keep Debug ID preparation dry-run-first and explicit; mutation of build output should require a `--write`-style opt-in and should never touch runtime source packages.
- Add runtime docs/API fields for optional `debugId` or `artifactId` only after ingestion and issue lookup can consume them.
- After backend intake exists, extend the Vite/Next/React Native proof apps to upload artifacts to the backend-compatible local intake, emit release/environment/service/debug-ID-bearing minified errors, and verify backend-unminified output; then add optional build-plugin or framework-owned upload layers.
- Add native follow-up contracts for Swift/Kotlin/Unity/C/C++/Objective-C symbol formats instead of claiming crash symbolication from normal error capture.
